decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost, aggressive creatures combined with spells that can provide additional damage or disruption to quickly overwhelm the opponent. Cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear can deal damage early on, while spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage can remove blockers or directly damage the opponent. The deck's mana base ensures it can cast its spells efficiently, while cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics provide flexibility in dealing with threats. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and end the game quickly.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful combo utilizing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II as key pieces. The deck includes cards that help protect and support these combo pieces, such as Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Iron-Shield Elf. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push provide disruption and removal, while cards like Lively Dirge and Cling to Dust offer additional utility. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's strategy, including lands like Bleachbone Verge, Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire, and Godless Shrine. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently to overwhelm opponents and secure victory.

The focus of this deck is to utilize the Greasefang Parhelion combo to quickly assemble a formidable board presence and overwhelm the opponent. By combining cards like Seasoned Hallowblade, Monument to Endurance, and Sheltered by Ghosts, the deck aims to protect its key pieces while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the combo by providing the necessary colors of mana to cast the various spells and abilities.

The deck focuses on utilizing efficient and aggressive creatures like Badgermole Cub and Ouroboroid to pressure the opponent. It also includes disruptive elements such as Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Collected Company allows for explosive turns by putting multiple creatures onto the battlefield. The deck aims to establish board presence quickly and overwhelm the opponent while using cards like Michelangelo's Technique and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to close out the game. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's aggressive strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage and board presence. It includes efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, as well as disruptive spells like Duress. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value by blinking permanents. The deck also features a variety of threats and card advantage engines, such as Thundertrap Trainer and Clearwater Pathway, to close out the game. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to efficiently set up a powerful combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II. By utilizing cards like Godless Shrine, Monument to Endurance, and Thoughtseize, the player can control the board while building towards this game-winning combo. Additional cards like Fatal Push and Duress help with disruption and removal, while Vanishing Verse and Bitter Triumph offer versatile answers to a variety of threats. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a devastating finish.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay, utilizing creatures with haste and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage help to maintain pressure on the opponent, while spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike serve as direct damage to finish off the opponent's life total. The inclusion of Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional sources of damage, making it difficult for opponents to stabilize. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with quick and efficient aggression.

This Red Deck Wins aggro deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells. The deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent, utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Kumano Faces Kakkazan to chip away at the opponent's life total while using burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike to finish them off. The inclusion of cards like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage help deal with opposing threats, allowing the deck to maintain its aggressive tempo and secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful control tool, allowing the player to access a wide range of spells and threats from their deck. The deck includes a variety of multicolor lands to support Niv-Mizzet's ability, as well as diverse removal options like Lightning Helix and Maelstrom Pulse. The addition of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion provides additional value and flexibility in longer games. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through a combination of powerful cards and strategic decision-making.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on deploying smaller creatures quickly and efficiently to apply early pressure on the opponent, while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The deck aims to maintain a steady stream of threats with cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition, while utilizing lands like Abandoned Air Temple and Boseiju, Who Endures to maximize its aggressive potential. The inclusion of cards like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Michelangelo's Technique provide additional options for closing out games quickly.

This deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing for explosive plays with cards like Scapeshift and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods. The deck also includes a variety of lands and ramp cards to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana. Additionally, the deck includes strategic control elements like Morlun, Devourer of Spiders and Aftermath Analyst to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own combo pieces. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells.

The primary focus of this deck is to establish control over the game by utilizing a variety of versatile cards such as counterspells, board wipes, and card advantage engines. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while gradually pulling ahead in card advantage and overall board presence. By efficiently managing resources and carefully timing impactful spells, the UW Control deck seeks to outvalue and outmaneuver the opposing player, ultimately leading to a favorable position and securing the victory.

This deck focuses on playing low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger, coupled with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their game plan. The inclusion of cards like Riverpyre Verge and Stormchaser's Talent provides versatility and card advantage to help maintain pressure on the opponent. The deck's strategy revolves around applying constant pressure and burning out the opponent's life total quickly.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively attack the opponent with fast and efficient creatures while utilizing burn spells and land abilities to finish off the opponent quickly. The deck aims to apply consistent pressure and deal damage swiftly, overwhelming the opponent before they can stabilize or mount a defense. By combining creatures with direct damage spells and land abilities, the deck aims to maintain the tempo and momentum to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a combination of cheap, fast creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. It also includes card draw and mana fixing spells like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics to maintain tempo and consistency. The deck aims to apply pressure early on and push for a quick victory by overwhelming the opponent with a mix of creatures, burn spells, and efficient resource management.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through hand disruption spells like Duress and Thoughtseize, as well as removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. It also utilizes creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to control the board. The mana base is set up to support the deck's strategy, with access to various colors of mana and utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures. Overall, the deck aims to control the game by removing threats and disrupting the opponent's plans.
